creative_brief: Clean, minimal design with a subtle countdown or progress-bar visual near the CTA suggesting the VIP list nearing capacity. Warm palette, single clear button. No imagery clutter — let the copy carry urgency.
subject: If 100 people join this week, we open the round
preheader: The VIP list is almost full. Here's what that means for you.
body:
Hi there,

I'll keep this simple because the maths does the talking.

If we hit our VIP target this week, we open the equity crowdfunding round on Birchal. If we don't, we wait — and the first-access window closes for everyone on the list right now.

You're already here. You already know what FarmThru is building: a farm-direct grocery store that cuts the 2,400km supermarket supply chain down to a straight line between regenerative farms and your local hub.

No middlemen inflating prices. No mysterious "best before" dates from six weeks ago. Around 15–20 partner farms across NSW, connected directly to you through our Brookvale hub.

But knowing isn't the same as owning.

The VIP list exists for one reason — to give people like you first access when the round opens. Priority updates. Exclusive Q&A with me. And a front-of-line position that disappears once we go public.

Your $5 refundable deposit holds your spot. That's it. No lock-in. No obligation to invest.

But here's the part I can't control: when the list fills, it fills. I won't reopen it. The people on it get first access to invest from just $50. Everyone else waits.

I started FarmThru because I couldn't answer a simple question — where does this food actually come from? Now we have the farms, the hub, and the model. What we need is people who want to own a piece of it.

This week matters. If you've been sitting on this, now is when sitting costs you something.

Join the VIP list →

No commitment beyond $5 (fully refundable). Order groceries when you want. Collect from Brookvale Monday to Friday. And if you choose to invest, you'll be part of building something the big chains will never offer.

This is not financial advice. Consider seeking independent financial advice. Full details in the disclosure document on Birchal.

Rachel & the FarmThru team
